fact: December 31 is exactly 10 days after the winter solstice, which means days in the Northern Hemisphere are already getting longer again - but you can't really tell yet!
In Antarctica, December 31 falls smack in the middle of summer, so penguins are having beach parties while you're bundled up in winter coats!
December 31 is the 365th day of the year (366th in leap years), making it literally the last chance for anything to happen that year - no pressure!
Tree sap moves so slowly in late December that it's basically frozen maple syrup inside the trunk - trees are like giant popsicles!
The sun sets earlier on December 31 than it did on the winter solstice in most places, even though days are getting longer - weird but true!
Hibernating animals like bears have been asleep for weeks by December 31, and their heart rate drops so low they're basically living teddy bears in slow motion!
In the Southern Hemisphere, December 31 is peak summer, so while you're shivering in the north, kids in Australia are having barbecues and going to the beach!
December 31 falls during Capricorn season in astrology, represented by a goat that climbs mountains - pretty fitting for climbing toward a new year!
Snow crystals typically form differently in the super-cold air around this date, creating more needle-like shapes instead of the pretty six-pointed stars you see earlier in winter!
Evergreen trees like pine and spruce are working overtime on December 31, using their waxy needles to prevent water loss in the dry winter air - they're basically wearing natural lip balm!
December 31 happens during the darkest stretch of winter in northern regions, when even your shadow looks tired and weak because the sun hangs so low in the sky!
